title: Verification cracks as agents trade and police surveil themselves - TCR 04/26/26
description:
Anthropic's Project Deal experiment exposes the agent quality gap in a controlled marketplace, the Metropolitan Police uses Palantir AI to surface a year of internal misconduct in 7 days, and Sullivan & Cromwell apologizes for AI-fabricated citations caught by opposing counsel.
This episode covers Project Deal's 186 agent transactions, the Met Police Palantir deployment, the UK's hundredfold AI datacentre emissions discrepancy, Google's $40 billion Anthropic commitment, the Cohere-Aleph Alpha $20 billion sovereign-AI merger, OpenAI's enterprise software talent raid, and the verification infrastructure forming across law, policing, climate planning, and agentic commerce.

https://sharedsapience.com/the-century-report-april-26-2026/

0:00 Open and framing
0:51 Anthropic Project Deal and the agent quality gap
10:00 Sullivan & Cromwell AI-fabricated citations
13:22 Cohere-Aleph Alpha sovereign AI merger
16:19 The other side of this
18:50 Verification stack failure across institutions

Anthropic's Project Deal ran 69 employees through an internal marketplace where AI agents completed 186 real transactions worth over $4,000, and the participants represented by lower-capability agents received objectively worse outcomes without perceiving the asymmetry. The Met Police pointed Palantir-built AI at its own officers for seven days, producing three arrests, 98 misconduct assessments, 500 prevention notices, and 42 senior officers flagged for attendance fraud. Sullivan & Cromwell filed a federal bankruptcy brief containing AI-fabricated case citations that opposing counsel discovered, despite the firm's formal AI policies and mandatory training. The UK's Department for Science revised its AI datacentre emissions projection more than a hundredfold within 24 hours after Guardian journalists exposed an order-of-magnitude gap with the Department for Energy. Google announced up to $40 billion in cash and compute for Anthropic at a $350 billion valuation, completing the hyperscaler triangle, while Cohere and Aleph Alpha announced a $20 billion sovereign-AI merger backed by Schwarz Group's STACKIT cloud. OpenAI began aggressively recruiting senior enterprise software executives from Salesforce, Snowflake, and Datadog as the contested frontier shifts from capability to distribution.
